focus: domain action names vs UI event handlers
design-system public API naming and alias precedence
ignore: low-level DOM passthrough props such as onClick and onChange
repo-wide rename suggestions outside the requested scope

high: a public component API name misrepresents controlled state semantics and callers are likely to wire it incorrectly
medium: callback prop names couple an app-level event API to a DOM event and the component already supports or is likely to support multiple interaction paths
internal functions mix domain actions and event adapter responsibilities in a way that hides where behavior should change
a design-system component introduces a legacy-style public prop where an established canonical prop exists, or an alias overrides the canonical prop
a design-system compound subcomponent is exposed as a separate public name even though it only makes sense under the main component namespace
low: renaming improves consistency and scanability but current behavior and API risk are limited
